revision_summary: Three targeted fixes required: (1) CLASSIFICATION CORRECTION — cherry_pick_moments[3]: change classification from 'knowledge_gap' to a thin-interest-answer / depth-probe-missed label; the schema 'knowledge_gap' directive refers to the rep lacking an answer, not the customer giving a vague one. Coaching implication is directionally correct but the label misfires. (2) BUTTON_UP STRUCTURAL GAP — cherry_pick_moments[4] and coaching_focus must be expanded to flag the complete absence of all five button_up required elements (appointment_datetime_confirmed, one_hour_block_stated, phone_appointment_specified, no_driving_at_computer, is_that_fair_handshake, text_followup_promise), not just the wrong-name error. The name error is a minor note; the structural button_up failure is a priority coaching beat that is currently buried. (3) INTRO FRAMING OVERCLAIM — rep_intent.evidence[1] and trajectory_verdict.grounded_examples[1] overstate the legitimacy failure by ignoring the partial company framing at 00:19 ('My job here at Ion is just to put together this design'). Revise to: the Tesla/state/certified-installer anchor was missing, but Ion was named and a design-framing was offered — the gap is the specific legitimacy template, not a total absence of company context. Also add missed_moment for 00:12 (Don's fragmented 'just it it, continue' as a distraction/disorientation signal) and for 00:19 (partial positive on curiosity framing). Tone correction on trajectory_verdict.summary: replace 'never got off the ground' with language that reflects the operational blocker as the proximate cause of truncation, not rapport failure.
